title: Rival Runs His Mouth
description: Your biggest rival just posted about how he's going to shut you down this Friday.

choices.flavor: Your clap-back goes viral at school. Coach is not thrilled, but the team loves it.

choices.text: Let your play do the talking
choices.flavor: You keep your head down. Your teammates respect the quiet confidence. Friday's game is going to be personal.
You text him a friendly message. He's confused but maybe you've found an ally instead of an enemy.

title: Rival on Opposite Team This Week
description: You just found out you're facing your biggest rival this Friday. He's been waiting for this matchup.

choices.flavor: You spend every spare moment watching his clips. By game day, you know exactly what he's going to do before he does it. Knowledge is power.
You're flying around at practice, hitting harder than ever. Your teammates feed off your energy. Coach loves it.
You refuse to let emotion cloud judgment. You're ready, but you're cool about it. That's a sign of a real competitor.

title: Rival Spreads a Rumor About You
description: Your rival started a false rumor at school about your recruiting interest. It's spreading.

choices.flavor: You corner him in the hallway. Things get heated. Everyone's talking about it for days, but not in the way you wanted.
You refuse to give it energy. Your play on the field speaks louder than any rumor. Scouts are watching you, not listening to gossip.
Your honest response gets reshared by local sports accounts. People respect you for handling it with class.

title: Your Rival Got Injured
description: The rival who's been getting under your skin just suffered a season-ending injury.

choices.flavor: You text him condolences. He's shocked but grateful. You might have just made an ally out of a rival. Real respect.
You feel relief. Now you can focus on dominating without him in your way. The path is clearer.
You use this as fuel. You want to show that you earned your spot, not just benefited from his injury. No asterisks.

title: Your Rival Gets Promoted to Varsity
description: The freshman you've been competing with just made varsity. Now you're finally going head-to-head for real.

choices.flavor: You bring extra intensity when he's on the field. The competition pushes you both to be better. Coach is loving this.
You take him under your wing. Now you're the veteran. Your growth mindset impresses the whole team.

choices.text: Challenge him to take your spot
choices.flavor: You make it clear: if he wants the starting spot, he's got to earn it from you. The battle is on.

title: You Sprain Your Ankle Bad
description: During practice, you roll your ankle hard. The trainer says it's a bad sprain. You're not sure you can play Friday.

choices.flavor: You tape it up and take the field Friday. You're moving slower, but you're out there. Your toughness is legendary.
You sit out. It kills you to watch from the sideline, but you know the right move. You'll be back stronger.
You test it in practice Thursday. The ankle feels better but not 100%. Coach makes the final call, and you respect it.

title: Hard Hit Leaves You Seeing Stars
description: You take a brutal shot to the head in practice. Everything spins for a moment. The trainer pulls you out to check for concussion.

choices.flavor: You tell the trainer you're fine. You're not fine. This headache won't go away, and you're foggy in class all week.
You report everything honestly. You're sidelined for two weeks. It's frustrating, but your brain matters more than Friday's game.

choices.text: Work with the trainer to return safely
choices.flavor: You follow the gradual return-to-play protocol. You're back in limited capacity by game day. Cautious but ready.

title: Your ACL Just Went
description: Non-contact injury during a drill. You heard the pop. You know it's bad. The trainer's face confirms it.

choices.text: Get surgery and commit to rehab
choices.flavor: You have surgery Monday. Rehab is brutal, but you show up every single day. This is your comeback story.

choices.text: Wait and see if it feels better
choices.flavor: You hesitate. Days turn into weeks. The injury gets worse. You should have acted faster.

choices.text: Focus on mental game while recovering
choices.flavor: You attend every practice as a mentor. You study film obsessively. You're still leading, just from the sideline.

title: Your Shoulder Has Been Aching
description: That shoulder injury from last season never fully healed. It's nagging you every game. Should you get it looked at?

choices.flavor: You tape it and move on. Games are fine, but that dull pain is always there in the back of your mind.
The specialist says rest and PT. You miss a few games, but you come back stronger and pain-free. Worth it.
You channel the frustration into your workouts. Playing with pain makes you tougher. You thrive on it.

title: College Scout Watching Practice
description: You notice a coach with a clipboard on the sideline. He's taking notes on you. This is real.

choices.flavor: You fly around practice making every play. The scout's taking lots of notes. You're trying to impress and it shows.
You focus on doing your job exactly right. Clean technique, smart decisions. This is film a coach wants to see.
You pretend he's not there. You play your game. Scouts respect composure, and that's what they got.

title: Your Dream School Offers You
description: The mail finally came. That envelope from your dream school is right there. Your hands are shaking.

choices.flavor: You call the coach and commit right there. The weight lifts off your shoulders. You're going to be a college player.

choices.text: Take time to think and compare offers
choices.flavor: You tell them you're interested but need to see other options. Smart move. Now other schools come calling.

choices.text: Wait to see if better programs offer
choices.flavor: You put it on hold, hoping a bigger name program reaches out. Maybe that was a mistake. The waiting is torture.

title: Visit Campus Visit Weekend
description: Your top school invited you for an official visit. You're flying out Friday to see the campus and meet coaches.

choices.text: Impress everyone at the combine workout
choices.flavor: You dominate the workout. Your numbers are elite. Every coach in the room is talking about you.
You spend time with the current team. They love you. You can see yourself there. This place feels like home.
You ask about graduation rates and support staff. The coaches respect that you're thinking long-term, not just glory.

title: Top Program Stops Recruiting You
description: The school you thought was your lock just told your coach they're moving on. They found someone bigger.

choices.flavor: You're motivated. Time to show them they made a mistake. Your film over the next month is unreal.
You reach out to programs that ARE interested. Turns out there are plenty of schools who want you.
Doubt creeps in. If they don't want you, maybe you're not that good. This hits harder than you expected.

title: Coach Wants You to Switch Positions
description: Coach pulled you aside. He thinks you'd be better at a different position. It's an opportunity, but it's scary.

choices.flavor: You start learning the new position. You're behind the curve, but you're willing to grind. Coach respects your trust in him.
You don't rush the decision. You ask smart questions. You want to understand the why before you commit.

choices.text: Refuse and stay where you are
choices.flavor: You tell him no thanks. You're solid where you are. Coach looks disappointed. You've just made an enemy of your position coach.

title: Better Program Wants You to Transfer
description: A powerhouse school reached out. They want to take you as a transfer. This is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.

choices.flavor: You're transferring. It's a big move leaving your team, but the opportunity is too big. You're going to play for a winner.

choices.text: Stay loyal to your current team
choices.flavor: You tell them no. Your teammates depend on you. You finish what you started. Loyalty builds legacy.

choices.text: Ask them to wait until next year
choices.flavor: You want to graduate from your current school. You ask if they'll still want you next year. They say they will.

title: Coach Threatens to Bench You
description: Coach called you out in front of the team. Said if you don't fix your attitude, you're coming off the field.

choices.flavor: You take it to heart. Next practice you're locked in. Coach notices. By Friday, you're playing your best football.
You mouth off. Huge mistake. Coach benches you the next game. Teammates think you're selfish. This is damage control time.

choices.text: Ask him what he needs to see
choices.flavor: You ask what changes he wants. He respects you for taking it seriously, not getting defensive. Now you know exactly what to fix.

title: Coach Praises Your Film in Front of Team
description: Coach shows your highlights to the whole team. He's using you as the example of how to play the position right.

choices.text: Let it motivate you to keep growing
choices.flavor: You feel validated but hungry. You're not satisfied. You want to be better next week. This is just the start.
You peak early. Next week you're not focused. Teams figure you out. Coaches get a taste of you thinking you've arrived.

choices.text: Use it to build your teammates up
choices.flavor: After practice, you pull up that younger guy and show him what coach was talking about. Now you're a mentor, not just a player.

title: Your Rival Just Broke Your School Record
description: The guy you've been competing with all season just posted the best stats in school history. And he did it in a game you sat out with injury.

choices.flavor: You're laser-focused. Every single snap you're trying to top what he did. When you break it, the crowd goes wild.
You post something respectful. It surprises everyone, including him. Real recognize real, even in rivals.

choices.text: Stay quiet and let your season speak
choices.flavor: You don't say anything. You just keep working. Records aren't won in a moment; they're built over time.

title: Your Rival Got a Scholarship Offer First
description: You just heard through the grapevine that your rival committed to a big program. They haven't offered you yet.

choices.text: Contact schools before they move on
choices.flavor: You reach out to coaches directly. You're not waiting. That fire in your belly is motivating calls to every program that matters.
You dominate these next few games. The film speaks for itself. Better late than never-coaches see you and start calling.
Maybe he is better. Maybe you're overrated. These thoughts spiral. You need to get your head right fast.

title: Your Rival Texts You Before the Big Game
description: He wants to grab food with you the night before the rival game. Says he wants to 'squash the beef' before Friday.

choices.flavor: You talk about college plans and life. Turns out you have a lot in common. He's a good dude off the field. Game day is still personal, but it's respect.
You tell him you need your head in the game. Friday you can talk. He respects the focus. The game is extra intense.

choices.text: Go but treat it like scouting
choices.flavor: You go but ask him film questions. You're reading his prep, his mindset. This is intelligence gathering wrapped in friendship.

title: Rival's Coach Disrespects Your Program
description: You overhear your rival's coach in the hallway talking trash about your team's defense. His words are designed to reach you.

choices.text: Tell your teammates and rally them
choices.flavor: You gather the team and repeat what you heard. The room goes silent, then fires up. Now everyone's personal about it.
You approach him and tell him his words are weak. He's shocked you had the guts. Respect and tension all at once.

choices.text: Let your play do the responding
choices.flavor: You don't say anything. Friday, you shut down their offense completely. The scoreboard is your response.

title: Your Best Teammate Got Seriously Injured
description: Your guy went down hard in practice. ACL, torn ACL. He's out for the season. You watched it happen.

choices.flavor: You sit with him in the trainer's room. You let him know he's got your support no matter what. He needed that.

choices.text: Play harder the next game in his honor
choices.flavor: You wear his number in a different way. Every snap is for him. The team rallies around this. You're playing with purpose.

choices.text: Keep distance and focus on yourself
choices.flavor: You're uncomfortable around injury. You keep your distance. Teammates notice. It feels cold, even if you don't mean it that way.

title: Cleared to Play But Still Hurting
description: The trainer says you're good to go. Medically cleared. But that shoulder still aches. It's not injured, it's just... sore.

choices.text: Play and push through the pain
choices.flavor: Your pain tolerance is off the charts. You play the whole game. By the end you're limping, but you're still standing.
You ask about taping, PT, anti-inflammatories. There's a plan to manage it safely. Smart player. Smart body.

choices.text: Overthink the pain and get cautious
choices.flavor: Every time you move you're worried about reaggravating it. You're hesitant. It affects your play. Worry is real too.

title: You Twisted Your Ankle Just Walking to School
description: Of all the ways to get hurt, you twisted your ankle on the sidewalk. Going to school. Not even at football.

choices.flavor: You tape it up and show up. Coach sees your commitment. But that ankle is swelling. You're limping hard by the end of practice.
One day off is worth a month of playing healthy. You ice it all day. Smart investment in the future.

choices.text: Laugh it off but be careful
choices.flavor: You make jokes about it all day. At practice you modify drills. Your teammates think it's funny. You stay smart about recovery.

title: Small School Offers Full Ride, Dream School Silent
description: A smaller D1 program offered you a full scholarship. Your dream school? Still no offer, but you're on their radar.

choices.flavor: You're going to college. Full ride. Guaranteed education. Playing time. This is the smart move, and you know it.
You tell the small school to wait. You believe your dream school is coming. The gamble is on. Waiting is torture.
You take both visits seriously. You're making an informed decision, not just chasing logos. That's maturity.

title: Your Recruiting Ranking Dropped After Bad Game
description: You had a rough game. Real rough. Now recruiting sites dropped your ranking. A star became three stars.

choices.text: Use it to prove them wrong
choices.flavor: You're on a mission. Next three games are your film. You're going to make them regret dropping you from their big boards.

choices.text: Ignore the rankings and play your game
choices.flavor: Rankings are just noise. You focus on what you control: effort, technique, preparation. The real evaluation happens on the field.
You spiral. If the experts dropped you, maybe you're not as good as you thought. This negative energy is poison.

title: Your Parents Can't Agree on Which College
description: You have three offers. Mom wants the academic school. Dad wants the best football program. You're caught in the middle.

choices.flavor: You listen to both, then tell them it's your future. You choose the school that feels right for YOU. They respect that maturity.

choices.text: Find a school that's both strong
choices.flavor: You identify a school with both academic prestige and a winning program. Both parents feel heard. Everyone's happy.
You punt the decision. They figure it out eventually, but you feel like you lost agency. This is your life to lead.

title: National Signing Day-You're Not Signed Yet
description: It's signing day. Every other top recruit is posting their announcements. You don't have an offer locked in yet.

choices.flavor: You know your value. A program will call. You don't chase. Within hours a coach reaches out. Patience paid off.

choices.text: Panic and accept the first offer
choices.flavor: You take the first scholarship that comes. Maybe it was the right call, maybe not. But the stress is over. Commit or regret-either way, you're done waiting.

choices.text: Use social media to stay visible
choices.flavor: You post highlights and your phone number. Coaches see you're pro-active. Interest picks up. Going viral works sometimes.

title: New Head Coach Just Got Hired Mid-Season
description: Your head coach was fired. New guy just walked in Tuesday. Completely different system, different culture.

choices.text: Buy in hard to the new system
choices.flavor: You're the first to embrace the change. You learn his playbook, his philosophy. He notices your adaptability. You become his guy.

choices.text: Resist and stick to old ways
choices.flavor: You think the old coach was better. You're not interested in learning new stuff. Conflict builds. This is not going to end well.

choices.text: Stay neutral and watch how it unfolds
choices.flavor: You observe. You see which guys get opportunities. You adjust when necessary. You're flexible, not stubborn or desperate to please.

title: Coach Just Named You Team Captain
description: Coach pulled you aside before practice. He's giving you the captain's armband. This is yours now.

choices.text: Lead by example on the field
choices.flavor: You earn the title with your play and work ethic. Guys follow because you're out there grinding. This is real leadership.

choices.text: Be vocal and hold guys accountable
choices.flavor: You're not afraid to call people out. You hold standards high. Some guys love it, some resent you, but respect is there.
You're too nice. You avoid conflict. Guys like you but you're not pushing anyone to be better. Captain should demand more.

title: Teammate Jealous You're Getting More Reps
description: You're getting more playing time. Your teammate who used to start has been cold toward you. He feels passed over.

choices.text: Address it directly and with respect
choices.flavor: You pull him aside. You tell him you respect his game and want him back in there. You're not the enemy. The conversation helps.
You don't say anything. You just keep outworking him. Eventually he either accepts his role or gets moved. No drama.

choices.text: Avoid him and keep the peace
choices.flavor: You pretend nothing's wrong. But locker room tension is real. It affects the whole team. Someone needs to deal with this.

title: Team Chemistry Is Falling Apart-You Need to Lead
description: Cliques forming. Players bickering. Coach is losing control. The locker room feels divided right before playoffs.

choices.text: Call a team meeting and unite everyone
choices.flavor: You stand up and tell the team they're better than this. You remind them why they play together. Guys respond. Unity returns.
You stay out of it. Coach tries to fix it but it's too late. By playoffs, the division is real. This haunts you.

choices.text: Just focus on winning your matchup
choices.flavor: You zone out the drama and focus on your assignment. At least you're doing your job. But the team's suffering.

title: Your Grades Are Falling-Eligibility Risk
description: Coach called you in. Your GPA just dropped below 2.5. You're at risk of losing eligibility. College scouts care about this too.

choices.text: Get a tutor and commit to recovery
choices.flavor: You hire a tutor. You cut social time. You grind. By mid-quarter you're back on track. The effort pays off fast.
You reach out to each teacher. Some help, some don't. It's a scramble but you're advocating for yourself. That matters.

choices.text: Stress about it but don't change anything
choices.flavor: You're anxious but procrastinating. Next progress report comes out and it's worse. Now you're really in trouble.

title: Prom Night vs. Big Game Prep
description: Prom is this Saturday. The biggest game of the season is Friday night. Your girlfriend wants you there. Coach expects you at the extra practice.

choices.flavor: You tell your girlfriend football comes first. It's brutal but you're all-in for this game. You both end up resenting it a little.

choices.text: Go to prom, show up to practice tired
choices.flavor: You go to prom, have an amazing night. Friday you're gassed and sloppy. Game's close but you're the reason it was close.

choices.text: Compromise: go to prom early, back by 11pm
choices.flavor: You show up for pictures and one dance. You head home, get sleep, and you're ready Friday. Everyone's happy. Smart balance.

title: Local Sports Journalist Wants to Interview You
description: The local newspaper reached out. They want to do a feature on your recruiting journey and rise as a player. Could be great exposure.

choices.flavor: You do the interview and talk honestly about your journey. The article is great. Coaches and teammates respect your openness.
You politely say no. You're not about the spotlight. Your teammates respect that. Focus stays on the field.
You hype yourself up in the article. Teammates think you're arrogant. Coaches expect more now. Expectations are sky-high.

title: Your Highlight Reel Goes Viral
description: Someone posted one of your plays on social media. It blew up. Thousands of shares, comments from Division 1 coaches.

choices.text: Embrace it and use it to build platform
choices.flavor: You start posting training content. Scouts follow you. You're building a personal brand. This is modern recruiting.
One play doesn't make a season. You keep your head down and let your work speak. The next play is just as important.
You can't stop posting. Next game you play like you're trying to recreate that one moment. You're chasing the high instead of grinding.

title: An Old Post of Yours Just Went Viral-Bad Way
description: Something dumb you posted two years ago is circulating. Out of context. Recruiters are seeing it.

choices.flavor: You delete it immediately and post that you regret it. The response is mixed but you handled it with maturity.
People forget things fast. You don't engage with it. You play well this week and new news takes over. It dies on its own.
You argue in the comments. More screenshots. More shares. The damage multiplies. This could affect recruitment.

title: Opportunity to Declare for Early Graduation
description: A college program is offering you a slot if you graduate early this spring. It's a guaranteed path to a DI school.

choices.flavor: You finish your senior year coursework early. You leave to join the college team in spring. It's surreal but this is the moment.

choices.text: Stay and finish senior year with your team
choices.flavor: You turn it down. Your teammates need you. You finish what you started. Senior year is yours to own.

choices.text: Ask if you can defer to next fall
choices.flavor: You ask if they'll hold the spot until you graduate normally. They agree. Now you have your cake and eat it too.

title: It's Down to Two Rival Schools
description: Two great programs want you. They're conference rivals. One is flashy and famous. One is stable and traditional. You have to choose.

choices.flavor: You go to the program everyone knows. The energy is electric. Playing there will be every kid's dream come true. Pressure is real though.
You choose the program with a track record of developing players. Less glamorous but you'll get better coaching and real development.
You demand both coaches sit down and explain what they want from you. Their answers guide your decision. This is about YOU, not the school's brand.

title: Big Test Tomorrow
description: You've got a major test coming up tomorrow. Do you study hard or skip to get extra practice in?

choices.flavor: You crack the books hard. Your brain feels sharper, and so does your on-field IQ. Education matters too.
You hit the field instead. Your technique improves, but you're stressed about that test. Coach notices you're distracted.

title: Teacher Offers Tutoring
description: Your English teacher pulls you aside and offers to tutor you. She's noticed you struggling in her class.

choices.flavor: You start meeting with her twice a week. Your grades climb. She's a tough but fair teacher who actually understands you.
You politely decline. More time for extra film study and conditioning, but your grade in her class keeps slipping.

title: Report Cards Are Out
description: Your report card came in the mail today. Time to face the music with your parents.

choices.flavor: Your grades are solid. Your parents beam with pride. Dinner is celebrating your achievement both on and off the field.

choices.text: Hide it and hope for the best
choices.flavor: You dread opening that envelope. Your grades are slipping and now you're stressed about your parents finding out. This never ends well.

title: You Made the Honor Roll
description: Congratulations! Your grades earned you a spot on the honor roll. There's an assembly to celebrate.

choices.flavor: You walk across that stage and the crowd cheers. Your name is in the paper. Respect comes from all angles, not just football.
You skip the assembly. Your quiet dedication speaks louder than any public recognition. Integrity is its own reward.

title: Homework Is Piling Up
description: Between practice, games, and film study, homework is stacking up fast. What do you do?

choices.flavor: You're up until 4 AM cranking through assignments. Your grades stay up, but you're exhausted and your body suffers.
Your teammate helps you organize your workload. You get it done, maintain your grades, and strengthen your friendship. That's leadership.
You blow it off to focus on football. Your grades drop and you get called out by your teacher. The temporary boost of confidence fades fast.

title: Group Project Partner is Slacking
description: Your partner on a group project isn't doing their share. The due date is in three days.

choices.flavor: You buckle down and finish the whole project solo. It's excellent, you get an A, but you're burned out. That's not sustainable.
You call them out directly. They get defensive, but they step up. Confrontation was uncomfortable but necessary.
You report the issue to the teacher. She separates the grades. You get full credit, but nobody at school likes a snitch.

title: Coach Warns About Grades
description: Coach pulls you aside: your grades are slipping. One more bad grade and you sit out. This is serious.

choices.flavor: You hit the books hard. Your grades climb back up and you stay eligible. No more close calls.
You copy answers on the next exam. You don't get caught this time, but the guilt eats at you. This path leads nowhere good.
You talk to your teachers about boosting your grade. They respect the hustle. You stay eligible and build real relationships.

title: Buddy Wants to Skip Class
description: Your best friend suggests skipping class to get extra field time. "Coach will love us for it," he says.

choices.flavor: You both slip out. The extra practice time is solid, but the attendance office calls your parents that evening. Consequences incoming.
You tell him no. He gives you grief, but you show up to class. School comes first. Football is the bonus.
You skip and get the extra practice, but your conscience kills you the whole time. The guilt outweighs the gains.

title: Prom vs. Big Practice
description: Prom is the same night as the critical playoff practice. Two weeks before the big games. What's your priority?

choices.flavor: You go in your tux and have the time of your life. Your date is happy. Coach is furious. But the memories last forever.
You show up to that practice in full gear. Coach respects the commitment. Your teammates feed off your focus. But you missed prom.
You go to prom for an hour, then leave for practice. You're exhausted, torn, and doing neither thing well. Pick a lane next time.

title: School Paper Wants Your Story
description: The school newspaper wants to interview you about football, college dreams, and balancing school.

choices.flavor: Your story runs front page. Suddenly people recognize you in the halls. The attention feels great and helps with recruiting.
You politely decline. You keep your head down and stay focused on your game. No distractions. That's maturity.

title: You Got Detention
description: You got detention for being late to class again. This is getting ridiculous.

choices.flavor: You show up, sit quietly, and do your time. The administrators respect that you accepted responsibility without complaint.
You make your case to the principal. Your charm works and they let you off. But you're building a reputation as a rule-bender.
Coach talks to the principal on your behalf. You avoid detention, but now you owe him one. That favor will come due.

title: Time for Your Driver's License
description: You're old enough to go for your driver's license now. Are you ready?

choices.flavor: You passed! Freedom never felt so good. Time to drive teammates to practice.
You decide to wait. No rush. Better to be confident than cocky when you're behind the wheel.
Cars can wait. Football cannot. You've got bigger priorities right now.

title: First Steps
description: Your little one just took their first steps across the living room!

choices.flavor: You toddle around the house all day. Nothing can stop those little legs.

choices.text: Be careful! Stay close to the couch
choices.flavor: Safety first. You learn to walk carefully and avoid bumps.

title: Daycare Drama
description: Another kid at daycare grabbed your favorite toy and won't give it back.

choices.flavor: You snatch the toy right out of their hands. Yours is yours.
The teacher makes them give it back. You learn that rules work.
You wander off and find something else. Sharing is hard at this age.

title: The Big Slide
description: The tallest slide at the playground looks both exciting and terrifying.

choices.text: Climb to the top and go for it
choices.flavor: WHEEE! The wind in your face is amazing. You climb right back up to do it again.

choices.text: Too scary, stay on the swings
choices.flavor: The swings are fun too. No need to push it yet.

title: Playing Catch
description: Dad wants to play catch in the backyard. He tosses a soft ball your way.

choices.text: Try your hardest to catch it
choices.flavor: You miss the first few, but eventually one sticks. Dad cheers. Best day ever.

choices.text: Throw it back as hard as you can
choices.flavor: Your throw goes sideways into the bushes. But the arm strength is there.

title: Football on TV
description: The family is watching the big game on Sunday. You're mesmerized by the players.

choices.flavor: You don't understand the rules yet, but something about the game clicks.

choices.text: Run around the living room imitating plays
choices.flavor: TOUCHDOWN! You spike a couch pillow. The family laughs and cheers.
The game is kind of boring. You drift off to the sound of commentary.

title: New Best Friend
description: A kid in your kindergarten class wants to play at recess. You've never talked to them before.

choices.flavor: You make a friend AND get exercise. Win-win. You two are inseparable at recess.
You pretend to be NFL players. Your friend is the quarterback. You're the star receiver.
You like your own company. Nothing wrong with that.

title: Recess Race
description: Some kids are lining up for a race across the field. Winner gets bragging rights.

choices.text: Sprint as fast as you can
choices.flavor: You give it everything. Whether you win or not, your legs feel like rockets.
You start slow but pass fading kids at the end. Smart strategy, even at this age.

choices.text: Skip the race, play on the jungle gym
choices.flavor: Climbing is more your thing. You hang upside down like a monkey.

title: Homework Time
description: Mom says it's time to do homework. Your favorite show is on TV.

choices.flavor: You knock it out fast. Mom is impressed. You still catch the end of the show.

choices.text: Watch the show, do homework later
choices.flavor: The show was great. The homework... not so much. Mom is not happy.

choices.text: Bargain: half now, half after the show
choices.flavor: Negotiation skills unlocked. Mom agrees to the deal.

title: The Bigger Kid
description: A bigger kid at school keeps pushing you around at lunch. Today they took your dessert.

choices.flavor: You puff up your chest and say NO. They're surprised. It doesn't go perfectly, but you earned respect.
The teacher handles it. The bully gets in trouble. System works.
You avoid the conflict. Smart, but it stings a little.

title: Youth Sports Signup
description: Sign-ups for youth sports are happening. Soccer, basketball, and swimming are all options.

choices.flavor: Your schedule is packed. You're exhausted but your body is developing fast.
Dedication pays off. You're the best kid on the team by the end of the season.

choices.text: Skip organized sports, play in the neighborhood
choices.flavor: Street rules. No refs, no coaches. Just kids playing until the streetlights come on.

title: New House
description: Your family is moving to a different part of town. New neighborhood, new school.

choices.flavor: New room, new friends to make. You're pumped. Change is good.
It's hard leaving your buddies. But you learn to adapt. That's a life skill.

title: Backyard Football
description: The neighborhood kids are playing football in someone's backyard. It's tackle, no pads.

choices.flavor: Your spiral isn't great yet, but a few passes connect. You feel like a pro.
You truck through kids left and right. A few scrapes, but totally worth it.
Nobody gets past you. The other kids start avoiding your side of the field.

title: Birthday Party
description: It's your birthday! Your parents ask what kind of party you want.

choices.text: Big party with the whole class
choices.flavor: Everyone shows up. Cake, games, presents. Best birthday ever.
Football, soccer, relay races. Your friends are exhausted but smiling.
Just your best friends. Video games, pizza, sleepovers. Quality over quantity.

title: School Talent Show
description: The school talent show is coming up. Some kids are singing, some dancing. What about you?

choices.flavor: You juggle a football on stage. The crowd goes wild. Future star energy.
You run the curtain and help other kids. The teacher calls you the MVP of the show.
Not your thing. You use the free time to play outside instead.

title: Report Card Day
description: Report cards are out. Your grades are... a mixed bag.

choices.flavor: You buckle down. The study habits you build now will pay off later.

choices.text: Whatever, grades don't matter for football
choices.flavor: You blow it off. Fun now, consequences later. Classic kid move.

title: Swimming Lessons
description: Mom signed you up for swimming lessons at the community pool.

choices.flavor: The water feels natural. Your lungs get stronger. Cross-training before you even know the word.
Cannonballs and splashing. The instructor is not amused, but you had fun.

title: Summer Camp
description: You're going to summer camp for a week. Cabins, campfires, and lots of outdoor activities.

choices.text: Be the most active kid there
choices.flavor: Hiking, swimming, capture the flag. You dominate every activity.

choices.text: Make as many friends as possible
choices.flavor: By the end of the week, everyone knows your name. Natural leader.

choices.text: Explore the woods on your own
choices.flavor: You find a cool creek and a frog. Sometimes solitude is the best adventure.

title: Wrestling in the Yard
description: Your older cousin challenges you to wrestle in the grass. He's bigger but you're quick.

choices.flavor: You're too fast to pin down. Your cousin can't believe it.
You get tossed around but refuse to quit. Toughness matters.

title: New Puppy
description: The family just got a puppy! It's your job to take care of it.

choices.flavor: Rain or shine, you and the dog hit the sidewalk. Responsibility builds character.
You throw the ball for hours. Your arm is getting stronger without even trying.

title: New Video Game
description: You got a new sports video game. Madden looks amazing.

choices.text: Play for hours, learn every play
choices.flavor: You memorize formations and routes. Your football brain is growing fast. Your eyes are tired.

choices.text: Play a little, then go outside
choices.flavor: Balance. A bit of Madden, then real football in the yard. Best of both worlds.

title: First Real Practice
description: Pads on. Helmet strapped. This is your first real organized football practice.

choices.text: Go full speed on every drill
choices.flavor: You're exhausted by the end but coaches noticed your hustle.

choices.text: Listen carefully and learn the plays
choices.flavor: While other kids goof off, you're absorbing everything coach says.

choices.text: Try to impress with big hits
choices.flavor: BOOM. You lay a kid out. Coach blows the whistle. 'Save it for Saturday, kid.'

title: Coach is Intense
description: Coach is screaming at the team after a sloppy practice. He singles you out for a mistake.

choices.flavor: You grit your teeth and run the drill again. Perfectly this time. Coach nods.
You're upset all practice. It shows. Coach benches you for the last drill.

choices.text: Ask coach to explain what you did wrong
choices.flavor: Coach is surprised by the maturity. He walks you through it one-on-one.

title: First Big Hit
description: A kid on the other team just ran full speed into you during a game. You're on the ground.

choices.flavor: You bounce up like nothing happened. The sideline erupts. Tough kid.

choices.text: Stay down for a second, then get up slowly
choices.flavor: You take a moment. Smart. No need to pretend that didn't hurt.

title: Same Position Rival
description: Another kid on your team plays the same position. Coach says only one of you starts.

choices.flavor: Extra reps, extra film, extra effort. By next game, the spot is yours.

choices.text: Be a good teammate and support them
choices.flavor: You cheer them on. Coach sees leadership. Both of you end up playing more.

choices.text: Complain to coach about playing time
choices.flavor: Coach doesn't appreciate the attitude. You ride the bench next game.

title: Championship Game Nerves
description: Tomorrow is the championship. You can't sleep. Your stomach is in knots.

choices.flavor: You picture yourself scoring the winning touchdown. By morning, the nerves are gone.

choices.text: Call a teammate to talk it out
choices.flavor: Turns out he's nervous too. You talk each other through it. Team bond.
You toss and turn but eventually drift off. Rest is rest.

title: Football Camp
description: You got invited to a week-long football camp with older kids. It's going to be intense.

choices.flavor: The coaches drill footwork, hand placement, and reads. You come back polished.

choices.text: Try to compete with the older kids
choices.flavor: You get pushed around but hold your own. The older kids start to respect you.

choices.text: Learn from watching the best players
choices.flavor: You sit and study the top players in every drill. Your football IQ jumps.

title: Three Losses in a Row
description: The team has lost three straight. Morale is low. Some kids want to quit.

choices.text: Rally the team in the huddle
choices.flavor: You speak up. 'We're not quitting.' The team rallies around your voice.

choices.text: Put your head down and grind harder
choices.flavor: Words are cheap. You let your effort do the talking.
You grumble on the sideline. It doesn't help anything.

title: The Play Everyone Talks About
description: Late in a close game, you make a ridiculous play. Parents are screaming. Teammates mob you.

choices.text: Celebrate big and soak it in
choices.flavor: You do a dance. The crowd loves it. Your name buzzes around town all week.
'We all did it.' Coach loves the maturity. Your teammates respect you even more.

title: Coach Wants to Move You
description: Coach thinks you might be better at a different position. He wants you to try it.

choices.flavor: New position, new perspective. You see the game differently now.

choices.text: Ask to stay at your current spot
choices.flavor: Coach respects your commitment. You double down on mastering what you know.

title: Twisted Ankle
description: You twist your ankle during practice. It hurts but you can walk on it.

choices.text: Tough it out and keep practicing
choices.flavor: You limp through the rest of practice. Gutsy but maybe not smart.
Rest now, play Saturday. The smart move. Your ankle thanks you.

title: Weight Room for the First Time
description: The older kids let you use the weight room after school. Everything is heavy.

choices.text: Start light and learn proper form
choices.flavor: The trainer shows you how to squat right. Foundation matters.

choices.text: Try to lift as much as the older kids
choices.flavor: You strain hard. Almost drop the bar. But you feel stronger already.

title: Grades Slipping
description: Your teacher calls home. Football is fun, but school is suffering.

choices.flavor: You start doing homework before practice. Grades recover. Mom is relieved.

choices.text: Football is more important right now
choices.flavor: Bad call. Mom grounds you from the next game. Lesson learned the hard way.

title: Exam Week Grind
description: Finals loom while your team enters bowl preparation. Balancing finals and football is brutal.

choices.text: Study all night, then hit practice hungover
choices.flavor: You barely pass, but you're a zombie on the field. Not your best week.

choices.text: Ask professor for extension and focus on team
choices.flavor: Professor gives you extra time. You ball out in practice. Win-win.

title: Professor Knows Your Name
description: Your psych professor stops you after class. Turns out she's a huge football fan.

choices.flavor: She tutors you. Your GPA actually goes up. Best outcome.

choices.text: Just chat about football and move on
choices.flavor: Cool conversation. You feel like a minor celebrity on campus now.

title: Party on Game Weekend
description: Your roommate throws a rager the night before a huge conference game.

choices.text: Stay in the room and rest
choices.flavor: You're locked in Saturday. 2 sacks, 12 tackles. Worth it.

choices.text: Join the party for a few hours
choices.flavor: You have fun but sleep isn't great. You still play okay though.

title: NIL Deal Offer
description: A local apparel company offers you decent money to wear their gear.

choices.text: Sign the NIL deal, easy money
choices.flavor: Nice. Your bank account is happier. Swag too.

choices.text: Decline and focus purely on football
choices.flavor: Scouts love the focus. Keep your head in the game, they say.

title: Campus Celebrity Status
description: You throw a game-winning TD pass. Now everyone knows your face.

choices.text: Embrace the celebrity, do interviews and appearances
choices.flavor: You're the man on campus. But your study time evaporates.

choices.text: Stay humble and let your game do the talking
choices.flavor: Scouts notice the maturity. You stay grounded. They like that.

title: Study Group Bonding
description: Three other athletes invite you to their study group. It's more hanging out than studying.

choices.flavor: Turns out studying with friends is actually fun. And effective.
You nail the test. Self-reliance builds strength.

title: Caught Partying Night Before Game
description: Coach's assistant sees you at a party Saturday night. He doesn't look happy.

choices.text: Own it at practice, run extra conditioning
choices.flavor: You run the gassers. Legs screaming. Coach respects the accountability.
Coach doesn't believe you. Trust is broken. Bad move.

title: Rivalry Week on Campus
description: Your school's biggest rival comes to town. The entire campus is electric.

choices.text: Feed off the energy, let it fuel you
choices.flavor: The crowd is insane. You're locked in. Monster game.

choices.text: Try to stay calm and methodical
choices.flavor: Clean execution. No errors. Controlled dominance.

title: Film Room Breakthrough
description: During film study, you see something nobody else sees. A key defensive tendency.

choices.text: Share the discovery with the whole team
choices.flavor: Your observation sparks the game plan. You're a leader now.

choices.text: Keep it to yourself and exploit it
choices.flavor: You feast on Sunday. Only you know why. Individual brilliance.

title: One-on-One Coach Feedback
description: Position coach pulls you aside after practice. Time for the real talk.

choices.text: Take the critique seriously, work on it obsessively
choices.flavor: You transform. Next week you're unrecognizable. Coach nods in approval.

choices.text: Nod along but do your own thing
choices.flavor: You ignore his advice. You plateau. Coach notices.

title: Conference Championship Intensity
description: Conference rival comes to your stadium for the championship game. Everything is on the line.

choices.text: Rise to the moment, dominate the biggest stage
choices.flavor: Scouts are watching. You put on a show. National stage performance.
No mistakes. Efficient. You win but don't blow anyone away.

title: Bowl Game Invitation
description: Your team is going bowling. It's a major bowl. TV deal, big stage, better opponent.

choices.text: Go all-in, prepare like it's the Super Bowl
choices.flavor: You're ready. Game time comes and you perform at your peak.

choices.text: Have fun with it, enjoy the bowl experience
choices.flavor: You loosen up. Good vibes. You play well, not great.

title: Spring Game Performance
description: Spring game is your chance to shine in front of pro scouts. You're hungry.

choices.text: Go off, put up huge numbers
choices.flavor: Scouts have you on film doing your thing. Impressive showing.
No turnovers, efficient play. Scouts see a smart decision-maker.

title: Two-A-Day Camp Grind
description: August two-a-days are coming. Eight hours of football in 100-degree heat.

choices.text: Attack it full speed, no mercy
choices.flavor: You're gassed but you're flying. Physical dominance.
Smart training. You avoid injuries and stay sharp all camp.

title: Playbook Mastery Moment
description: You finally get it. The entire playbook just makes sense now.

choices.text: Help younger players understand it too
choices.flavor: You're becoming a leader. Offense running smoother because of you.
You're the smartest guy in the room. Nobody out-thinks you.

title: NFL Scout at Practice
description: You spot a scout in the stands during your morning practice. He's got a radar gun and a clipboard.

choices.flavor: You're flying. Hits are crisp. He writes a lot of notes. Good signs.

choices.text: Stay focused on team reps, ignore the scout
choices.flavor: Scouts respect the focus. You don't chase individual stats.

title: Agent Contacts You Early
description: An agent slides into your DMs after your breakout performance. Says he represents three NFL guys.

choices.text: Meet with him, start planning for the draft
choices.flavor: He gives you intel on draft positioning. You feel more prepared.

choices.text: Wait and stay focused on school
choices.flavor: You're not ready yet. Smart to stay grounded. Focus on film.

title: Transfer Portal Temptation
description: A bigger program offers you the chance to transfer. More money, more fame, more scouts.

choices.flavor: Scouts like loyalty and commitment. You finish strong at your school.
More exposure. But new teammates don't know you. Mixed blessing.

title: Draft Stock Rising
description: You overhear coaches talking about your draft projections. Early second round, maybe late first.

choices.flavor: You're hungry now. Every rep matters. You accelerate your development.
You don't get ahead of yourself. Steady development. No distractions.

title: Sports Reporter Requests Interview
description: Local sports reporter wants to do a profile on you. TV segment, newspaper feature.

choices.text: Do the interview, tell your story
choices.flavor: The segment airs. You're on local TV. People recognize you now.

choices.text: Decline, stay out of the spotlight
choices.flavor: No distractions. You keep your eyes on the prize.

title: Senior Day Emotions
description: Senior Day arrives. It's your last home game at the college level. The weight hits you.

choices.text: Channel the emotions into an epic performance
choices.flavor: You leave everything on the field. One last time as a college player.
The moment overwhelms you. You play tight. Still decent but not your best.

title: College Legacy Discussion
description: Coach sits you down to talk about your legacy at the school.

choices.text: Commit to making a real impact in your remaining time
choices.flavor: You finish strong. Your name means something here forever.

choices.text: Think about your own NFL future instead
choices.flavor: You focus on draft prep. Legacy takes a backseat.

title: Veteran Gives You the Talk
description: A 10-year vet pulls you aside in the locker room. He wants to give you some real advice.

choices.text: Listen closely, take it to heart
choices.flavor: His wisdom shapes your rookie year. You avoid rookie mistakes.

choices.text: Thank him but do your own thing
choices.flavor: You've got your own style. You learn the hard way instead.

title: Young Rookie Asks for Help
description: A lost-looking rookie approaches you for advice in the facility.

choices.flavor: He's grateful. You become a leader. Locker room respects it.

choices.text: Brush him off, everyone figures it out
choices.flavor: You're busy with your own thing. Cold but effective.

title: Locker Room Argument
description: You and a teammate have it out after a loss. Frustration boils over.

choices.flavor: You squash it. Mutual respect afterward. Team chemistry better for it.

choices.text: Let it fester, don't back down
choices.flavor: Tension remains. Locker room is split. Bad vibes all week.

title: Chance to Speak in Team Meeting
description: Coach asks if anyone wants to address the team. Everyone's looking at you.

choices.text: Stand up and fire up the team
choices.flavor: Your words resonate. Team energy shifts. You're a captain now.

choices.text: Stay quiet, let someone else speak
choices.flavor: Safe choice. But missed opportunity to build your leadership.

title: Pregame Ritual Bonding
description: You start a ritual before games. Other guys want to join in.

choices.text: Make it a team thing, build unity
choices.flavor: The ritual becomes sacred. Team feels more bonded. It works.

choices.text: Keep it just for you, your own superstition
choices.flavor: Your ritual is your edge. Your performance improves.

title: Tough Press Conference Question
description: A reporter asks you a pointed question about a bad loss. The whole room is listening.

choices.flavor: Reporters respect your honesty. Fans see accountability. Good PR.
Everyone sees through it. Looks bad in the media.

title: Social Media Viral Moment
description: A highlight from your game goes viral. Millions of views overnight.

choices.text: Ride the wave, capitalize on the attention
choices.flavor: You're famous now. Endorsement deals start coming in.
One viral moment doesn't define you. You keep grinding.

title: National TV Spotlight Game
description: Your team is primetime Sunday night football. The world is watching.

choices.text: Rise to the moment, monster performance
choices.flavor: Everyone saw that. You looked like an All-Pro. Game changes your trajectory.

choices.text: Get caught up in the moment, play loose
choices.flavor: You're having fun but not locked in. Decent game, nothing special.

title: Podcast Interview Offer
description: A popular sports podcast wants you for a deep-dive interview. No PR team, just real talk.

choices.text: Do it, be authentic and real
choices.flavor: Listeners connect with you. Your stock goes up.
Safer route. You avoid saying something that gets twisted.

title: Sports Talk Radio Debate
description: You're on the radio and callers are debating your performance. Some love you, some say you're overrated.

choices.text: Ignore the noise, focus on play
choices.flavor: You tune it out. Critics are always there. You improve instead.

choices.text: Let it fuel you, prove them wrong
choices.flavor: Chip on your shoulder. You go off next week.

title: Endorsement Deal Offer
description: A major brand wants you for an endorsement. Real money. Your face on commercials.

choices.flavor: Bank account is looking good. You're set for life financially.

choices.text: Hold out for a bigger deal
choices.flavor: You negotiate hard. Get a better rate. Respect the hustle.

title: Financial Advisor Meeting
description: Your agent sets up a meeting with a financial advisor. Time to get serious about money.

choices.text: Listen closely, invest the money right
choices.flavor: Smart decisions early. You'll be rich long after football.

choices.text: Spend it on cars and jewelry
choices.flavor: You look good but you're not thinking long-term. Bad call.

title: Charity Event Invitation
description: A charity wants you to headline an event. Give back to the community, meet donors.

choices.text: Show up and give your time
choices.flavor: You help raise money for a good cause. People remember the kindness.

choices.text: Send a donation instead, skip the event
choices.flavor: Money talks. You help but stay focused on the season.

title: Contract Restructure Offer
description: Team wants to restructure your deal to free up cap space. Your agent says it's normal.

choices.text: Agree, show you're a team player
choices.flavor: Team appreciates the flexibility. You stay loyal. Everyone wins.
You hold firm. Agent negotiates hard. You win this one.

title: Agent Pushes for Trade Demand
description: Your agent thinks a trade would get you more money or better opportunity. He wants to start the process.

choices.text: Stay loyal, finish what you started
choices.flavor: You're not a quitter. Scouts and fans respect the loyalty.

choices.text: Request a trade, chase better situation
choices.flavor: New team, fresh start. Could work out great or be messy.

title: New Offensive Coordinator
description: The new coordinator wants to change everything. New plays, new philosophy. Total overhaul.

choices.text: Master the new system, be adaptable
choices.flavor: You learn fast. New scheme highlights your strengths. You thrive.

choices.text: Resist the change, stick with what works
choices.flavor: You're slow to adapt. Performance dips. Growing pains.

title: Film Study Reveals Weakness
description: You're studying your opponent's film and see a critical weakness that nobody's talking about.

choices.text: Share it with the team, be the game-changer
choices.flavor: Your find changes the game plan. You're responsible for the victory.

choices.text: Keep it quiet and exploit it yourself
choices.flavor: You're the only one who knows. You go off this Sunday.

title: Joint Practice with Rival
description: Your team has a joint practice with a division rival. Intensity is through the roof.

choices.flavor: Rival respects you now. You've got their attention.
Stay composed. Fights break out, but you stay clean.

title: Thursday Night Football Primetime
description: Your team is Thursday night primetime. Short week, national audience.

choices.text: Embrace the spotlight, play lights out
choices.flavor: Thursday night is your night. You're electric.

choices.text: Manage the short week, play smart
choices.flavor: You don't get caught up. Efficient play. Smart game management.

title: Bye Week Recovery or Training
description: Your bye week is here. Rest your body or keep training? The smart play is rest.

choices.flavor: You come back fresh. Mental reset too. Second half is your best football.

choices.text: Hit the extra training, get ahead
choices.flavor: You're still gassed from the first half. Good effort, risky.

title: Preseason Rep Management
description: Coach is limiting your snaps in preseason to keep you healthy. You want to play more.

choices.flavor: Smart coaching. You stay fresh for the season. No injuries.

choices.text: Push for more snaps, prove yourself
choices.flavor: You get more reps. Risk of injury but you feel ready.

title: Playbook Installation Stress
description: New playbook is complex. You're stressed about learning it all in time.

choices.text: Double down on film and study
choices.flavor: You're the first one in the facility. You master it. Confidence soars.

choices.text: Take it in stride, you'll figure it out
choices.flavor: Learning on the fly. You get it done but with some hiccups.

title: Family at the Game
description: Your family is in the stands for a big game. You feel their support.

choices.text: Play for them, dedicate the game to family
choices.flavor: You're flying. Post-game hug with mom is everything. Worth it.

choices.text: Block it out, focus on the play
choices.flavor: You separate emotion from performance. Controlled execution.

title: First Big Purchase
description: You're buying your first major thing as a pro. House, car, jewelry. Your moment.

choices.text: Buy smart, invest in real estate
choices.flavor: House in a good neighborhood. Building wealth. Long-term thinking.

choices.text: Flex on everyone, get the flashy stuff
choices.flavor: Your car is sick. Your chain is cold. Everyone knows you made it.

title: Injury Scare in Practice
description: You take a hit in practice. Something doesn't feel right. Could be nothing or could be serious.

choices.text: Report it, get checked out immediately
choices.flavor: Smart move. Doctor clears you but you avoided a bigger problem.

choices.text: Tough it out, don't be weak
choices.flavor: You keep playing. It gets worse. Now you're seriously hurt.

title: Teammate Gets Cut
description: A guy you've been grinding with all season just got released. Brutal reality of the business.

choices.flavor: You help him figure out his next move. Respect the brotherhood.

choices.text: Stay focused on your own career
choices.flavor: That's the league. You keep grinding. Survival of the fittest.

title: Pro Bowl Voting Announced
description: Pro Bowl voting results come in. You made it. First All-Pro nod.

choices.text: Embrace the honor, use it to motivate
choices.flavor: You're flying high. Rest of season is your best work.

choices.text: Grateful but stay humble, focus on team
choices.flavor: You keep it real. Team comes first. Scouts love the maturity.

title: Community Service Event
description: Team is doing a community service day. Build something with local kids.

choices.text: Show up and be fully present
choices.flavor: Kids light up. You're a hero to them. Worth every minute.

choices.text: Make an appearance, keep it brief
choices.flavor: You show face. Good for the brand but not deeply invested.

title: Old College Teammate in the League
description: You run into a college teammate at the facility. He made it too. Different division but same journey.

choices.text: Reconnect, grab dinner, share the journey
choices.flavor: You bond over the grind. Real ones recognize real ones.
You're both trying to make a name. Respect the competition.

title: Trade Deadline Media Circus
description: Your name is being floated in trade rumors. Media is everywhere. Is it real or just noise?

choices.text: Stay focused, let agent handle it
choices.flavor: You block out the noise. Deadline passes. You're staying. Huge relief.

choices.text: Let the rumors mess with your head
choices.flavor: You're distracted. Performance suffers. Not your best week.

title: Blitz Package Brings Heat
description: The defense is sending extra pass rushers every play. Your line is struggling to keep them off you.

choices.text: Step up in the pocket and deliver quick
choices.flavor: You trust your instincts. A few quick completions and they back off. Game is yours.
You absorb some punishment but keep playing smart. Your teammates notice your grit.
You break the pocket and make defenders look silly. Risk it for the biscuit.

title: The Red Sea Opens
description: Your offensive line just created a huge hole. You've got open field ahead and a choice to make.

choices.text: Hit it full speed for maximum yards
choices.flavor: You explode through the hole. 15 yards, maybe more. Your legs are feeling it though.

choices.text: Bounce it outside and follow your edge blocker
choices.flavor: You show great patience and burst to the edge. Textbook execution.

choices.text: Follow your instinct and cut it up
choices.flavor: You feel the secondary collapse and cut it back for even more yards. Vision like that is rare.

title: The Ball Slips Through
description: A perfectly thrown pass hits your hands and bounces away. Crowd is silent. This is on you.

choices.text: Shake it off and stay focused
choices.flavor: You dust yourself off. Next play, you make the catch. Growth in adversity.
You're mad at yourself. Maybe a bit too mad. It affects the rest of the game.
You communicate. He throws you another slant. This time, hands secure. Team connection.

title: Pancake Block of the Game
description: You lock onto your man and drive him into the turf. Crowd goes wild. This is domination.

choices.flavor: You get in the end zone and go wild. Bench loves it. Coach gives you a look.
You pancake him then extend your hand. That's a player with class.

choices.text: Stay focused and get next rep
choices.flavor: No celebration needed. You already know you're the best on the field.

title: Kick to Win the Game
description: Two seconds left. One timeout left. Your team down by 3. You're about to send everyone home happy or devastated.

choices.text: Block out all noise and drill it
choices.flavor: The crowd disappears. It's just you and the uprights. Pure. Straight. Good. You're the hero.

choices.text: Let the moment get to you
choices.flavor: Your hands shake. The kick drifts left. Game over. You'll replay this for months.
You've made this kick 1,000 times. This is just another rep. Boom. Good.

title: Homecoming Game - Full House
description: Tonight's homecoming. The stadium is absolutely packed. Every eye in town is on you.

choices.text: Rise to the occasion with dominant play
choices.flavor: The lights, the crowd, your hometown. You play the game of your life.
Too many eyes. You're tentative. Not your best game.

choices.text: Have fun and soak it in
choices.flavor: You remind yourself why you love this. Pure joy out there.

title: Senior Night Recognition
description: You're being honored before the game. Your parents are on the field. This is emotional.

choices.flavor: You play with everything you have. This night is forever.
The emotion is real. You're not quite yourself on the field.

choices.text: Make a statement for the scholarship scouts
choices.flavor: Your parents are watching. The scouts are watching. You leave nothing on the field.

title: Win Gets You In the Playoffs
description: This game is it. Win and you're in. The entire season comes down to these four quarters.

choices.text: Take control and lead your team
choices.flavor: You play like a leader. Everything you touch turns to gold. Playoffs, here you come.
No turnovers. Perfect execution. You did your job.
Everything feels off. You make bad decisions. Season ends here.

title: Prom Night vs. Game Day Practice
description: Prom is this Saturday. But there's a mandatory team practice session that same day. Your date is waiting for an answer.

choices.text: Go to prom and make your date happy
choices.flavor: Best night ever. Teammates are disappointed, but you have no regrets.
Your date is hurt but understands. Coach nods his approval. This is dedication.
You try to do both. It's awkward and neither goes great.

title: School Play Audition Offer
description: Drama teacher just asked if you'd audition for the spring play. Lead role. You're intrigued but football is your life.

choices.text: Give it a shot and audition
choices.flavor: You're on a different kind of stage now. Who knew you had range?
You have your priority. Football is the only stage you need.

choices.text: Get made fun of for even considering it
choices.flavor: Teammates make jokes. You laugh it off but the offer stings a bit.

title: Part-Time Job Opportunity
description: Local business just offered you weekend hours at good pay. Could help your family. But it cuts into training time.

choices.text: Take the job and grind both
choices.flavor: You're working and training. It's exhausting but you're providing.

choices.text: Turn it down for pure focus
choices.flavor: Football is your ticket. You're all-in on the grind.

choices.text: Take the job and slack off training
choices.flavor: You're earning money but losing fitness. Bad trade.

title: Downpour on Game Day
description: The rain is coming down hard. The field is soaked. Everything is slippery and weird. Adapt or die.

choices.text: Embrace it and control the ball
choices.flavor: You adjust immediately. Your technique is crisp. Everyone else is slipping.

choices.text: Struggle with the weather like everyone
choices.flavor: The conditions get to you. It's a sloppy game for everyone.

choices.text: Use the weather as an excuse
choices.flavor: Blaming the rain won't win games. You know better.

title: Snow Game - Historic Winter Storm
description: A foot of snow just dumped overnight. Game is still on. This is old-school football weather.

choices.flavor: This is your element. You move like the snow was made for you.
The cold is brutal. You're tough but not unbeatable.
Your hands are numb. Your mind is foggy. Not your day.

title: Extreme Heat and Humidity
description: 95 degrees, 90% humidity. The heat index is over 105. This is conditioning vs. skill today.

choices.flavor: In the 4th quarter, they're gassed. You're still flying.
You didn't hydrate enough. You're cramping. It's rough.
You pace yourself. You're the smartest player on the field.

title: Local Paper Front Page Feature
description: The high school newspaper just published a big feature on you. Your popularity is officially through the roof.

choices.text: Stay humble and let it fuel you
choices.flavor: The attention is nice but you let it motivate, not distract.

choices.text: Let it go to your head
choices.flavor: You're feeling yourself. Maybe a little too much. Cockiness is showing.
Everyone's eyes are on you now. The pressure is real.

title: Weird Pain During Practice
description: You're feeling something in your knee or ankle that wasn't there before. Nothing major... yet. Report it or push through?

choices.text: Report it to the trainer immediately
choices.flavor: You're smart about it. Trainer clears you. Better safe than sorry.

choices.text: Push through and don't say anything
choices.flavor: You're tougher than the pain. It probably goes away. Probably.

choices.text: Tell the coach and sit out practice
choices.flavor: You miss some reps but your body is ready for Friday.

title: Finals Week Hits Different
description: Three exams in four days. The library is your new home. Football feels secondary for the first time all semester.

choices.text: Ace the finals and still lift
choices.flavor: You manage both. It's exhausting but you're leaving nothing on the table.
Grades come first this week. Your body will forgive you.
You're scattered. School suffers. Football suffers. Not great.

title: Study Group Becomes Football Film Room
description: Your study group evolved into your team watching film. Teammates became your academic accountability partners too.

choices.text: Lean on each other, get better together
choices.flavor: Study buddies who also happen to be your teammates. Perfect.
Great bonding but not much studying happening.

title: Time to Declare Your Major
description: Registration closed today. You need to pick your major. This shapes your post-football life. What's it going to be?

choices.flavor: You're passionate about your major. School comes alive.
You have time to focus on ball. But is college just a stepping stone?
Practical. You're thinking long-term and beyond football.

title: Your Highlight Goes Viral
description: That catch/sack/run from Saturday? It's all over social media. You have 50,000 new followers. Welcome to the spotlight.

choices.text: Use it to build your brand
choices.flavor: You engage with fans. Your brand is growing. This is the NFL showcase.
One play doesn't define you. Next play matters more.

choices.text: Let it distract you with parties
choices.flavor: You're the life of the party now. Your performance suffers.

title: Brand Deal Falls Through
description: The endorsement you were counting on for cash? Company pulled out due to some social media post drama. Lesson learned.

choices.flavor: You're smarter about your brand now. Good lesson.
You don't care. Keep saying what you want. Probably not the move though.
You're quiet on social. Let the dust settle.

title: Fan Approach in Public
description: A kid just recognized you at the coffee shop and asked for a picture. His face is pure joy. This is the NFL dream.

choices.text: Make his day with genuine connection
choices.flavor: That kid will remember you forever. This is why you play.
Professional. Nice interaction.

choices.text: Act annoyed and brush him off
choices.flavor: One day you might regret how you treated people. Probably today.

title: Transfer Recruit Threatens Your Spot
description: New transfer from a Power-5 school just enrolled. He's gunning for your position. The battle is on.

choices.text: Compete harder than you've ever competed
choices.flavor: You bring everything. He brings everything. You come out on top.
You're nervous. It shows. Maybe you're not ready for this challenge.

choices.text: Mentor him and win the room
choices.flavor: You help him acclimate. Locker room respects your security. You keep your spot.

title: Your Moment to Lead
description: Team is flat after a loss. Coach looks at you. The room goes quiet. They want to hear from you.

choices.text: Give them a speech they'll remember
choices.flavor: Your words resonate. This team is going to war with you.
You say what needs to be said. Respectable.

choices.text: Stay silent and let the moment pass
choices.flavor: You weren't ready. The moment's gone. Someone else speaks up.

title: Friday Night Lights at the College Level
description: Ranked opponent. Night game. Packed stadium. The energy is electric. This is what you dreamed about.

choices.text: Feed off the energy and dominate
choices.flavor: This is your stage. You own the night.

choices.text: Play well but feel the pressure
choices.flavor: It's different with 70,000 screaming. You adjust.
The stage is too bright. You play timidly.

title: Rivalry Week - Pure Hatred
description: This is THE game. The one that matters most. Everything else is noise. You hate these guys.

choices.flavor: The rage is your fuel. You play flawlessly.

choices.text: Get too emotional and take penalties
choices.flavor: You want it too bad. Flags flying.
You let your play do the talking. Quieter but more devastating.

title: Bowl Game Selection Show
description: You just found out where you're going to play a bowl game. Cross-country trip. New city. Legacy moment.

choices.text: Use it as motivation to be great
choices.flavor: You're locked in. This bowl game is your Super Bowl.
New city is fun but training is secondary.

choices.text: Feel honored to represent your school
choices.flavor: You're proud. This is bigger than you.

title: NFL Scout Watches You in Practice
description: Coach tipped you off. There's an NFL scout on the sideline watching. This is a direct path to draft day.

choices.flavor: You play like you were born for this. He's writing notes the whole time.
You know he's there. It affects your game.

choices.text: Ignore him and play your game
choices.flavor: One scout doesn't change anything. You do you.

title: Combine Prep Training Begins
description: Season's over. Now it's time to train for the combine. Every fraction counts. 40-time, bench press, everything.

choices.text: Train like your draft stock depends on it
choices.flavor: You're a machine. By combine time, you're unstoppable.
You're burnt out. Maybe you should've stayed focused.
You're intelligent about your prep. Strong and injury-free come combine time.

title: Agent Wants to Represent You
description: An agent just cornered you after practice. He wants to represent you. Commission, guidance, connections. What's the move?

choices.text: Sign with him and get professional support
choices.flavor: You have someone in your corner now. Draft preparation just got serious.

choices.text: Take your time and talk to family first
choices.flavor: Smart move. You're making decisions with people you trust.
You agreed to representation. Hope he's legit.

title: Your Name in Trade Rumors
description: ESPN is reporting you're demanding a trade. You never said that. Your agent is getting calls. This is getting messy.

choices.text: Stay silent and let your agent handle it
choices.flavor: You don't feed the narrative. Your play does the talking.
The internet goes wild. Maybe keep it simple next time.

choices.text: Give a statement that you're committed
choices.flavor: You squash the narrative immediately. Class move.

title: Fumble on National TV
description: Monday night. Prime time. You just coughed it up at a critical moment. 10 million people just saw it.

choices.text: Bounce back and dominate the rest of the game
choices.flavor: You made a mistake and corrected it immediately. That's a pro.

choices.text: Get in your head and play tentatively
choices.flavor: One mistake snowballs. You're not yourself the rest of the night.

choices.text: Blame the lineman for the pressure
choices.flavor: You deflect. Locker room notices. Not a good look.

title: 4th Quarter Heroics
description: Two minutes left. Down 3. Crowd is deafening. You just got the ball. This is your moment.

choices.flavor: You're a legend. That moment defines your career. Legacy points.
You gave everything. Sometimes it's not enough. You'll be back.
You threw a pick. Or made a dumb decision. Haunts you.

title: Rookie Looks Up to You
description: The young guy in your position room is clearly watching everything you do. He wants to be you. This is a responsibility.

choices.flavor: You're teaching the next generation. This is legacy.
You're focused on your game. He'll figure it out.

choices.text: Test him and make practice hard
choices.flavor: You give him the old school treatment. Sink or swim.

title: Charity Event in Your Name
description: Your foundation is hosting a charity gala. Local kids are there. You raised six figures for the community.

choices.text: Show genuine care for the cause
choices.flavor: You're making a real difference. This means more than football.

choices.text: Show up and take photos for PR
choices.flavor: Nice headline. Good for the brand.
You needed the recovery. But the kids are disappointed.

title: Media Day Chaos
description: It's Super Bowl week. Media day is a zoo. Every reporter wants a piece of you. It's exhausting.

choices.text: Control the narrative with great sound bites
choices.flavor: You're quotable. Headlines are yours to control.
No headlines. No controversy. Just football.
You stir the pot. The internet is talking about you.

title: National Commercial Shoot
description: You're shooting a national commercial for a major brand. The paycheck is huge. Good for your family's future.

choices.text: Nail it and look like a star
choices.flavor: You're charismatic. Brand loves it. Commercial goes viral.

choices.text: It's awkward but you get paid
choices.flavor: Not your best work but the check cleared.

choices.text: Use it as a focus break from football
choices.flavor: Nice mental break. Your body might be grateful too.

title: Court-Ordered Community Service
description: Your off-field actions caught up with you. You're doing community service. Time to right the ship.

choices.text: Use it as a wake-up call
choices.flavor: You take it seriously. Actually helping people changes your perspective.
You show up. You get your hours. Nothing more.
That was stupid. Now there are real consequences.

title: Your Kid's First Football Game
description: Your son/daughter just played their first youth football game. They asked you to come. Career-high week. What's the priority?

choices.text: Be there and soak up every moment
choices.flavor: You watched them shine. They'll remember this forever. So will you.
You tried. Missing the first half hurts though.
They understood. Didn't mean it was OK.

title: Blown Call Costs You the Game
description: The ref made a terrible call in the 4th quarter. Everyone knows it. Game's lost. You want justice. Twitter is on fire.

choices.text: Call it out in the presser
choices.flavor: You say what everyone's thinking. League fines you. Worth it.
You bite your tongue. Next time it goes your way.
You're taking responsibility. Shows character even if the call was bad.

title: Retirement Whispers in the Media
description: You're in your 30s. Some beat writers are saying you might hang it up. Are they right? Do you have more left?

choices.text: Play like you have something to prove
choices.flavor: You're motivated. Best performance of the year coming up.
The writing's on the wall. Maybe it's time.

choices.text: Ignore it and play your game
choices.flavor: One day at a time. No retirement talk.

title: Hall of Fame Conversation Starts
description: A reporter just asked if you think you'll make the Hall of Fame. It's real. Your career is legendary.

choices.flavor: You're thinking about Canton. This is the ultimate validation.

choices.text: Stay humble and focus on now
choices.flavor: It's premature. One season at a time.
Your legacy is on the line. You're giving it everything.
